hypothesis: Extending the single-objective reactor yield optimization to multi-product systems, incorporating catalyst composition (e.g., silver-copper alloy ratio) as an additional Bayesian optimization parameter alongside the six electrolyzer operating parameters will reveal synergistic optima where the bicarbonate-to-carbonate ratio simultaneously tunes in situ CO2 partial pressure to favor C–C coupling, enabling a reactor yield metric defined as the joint product of C2+ partial current density and CO2 utilization to exceed the sum of individually optimized single-product reactor yields by at least 30% within fewer than 60 automated experiments.
